title: Elementify Project Profile, Sensorik, and Approval Hardening Plan
reason: Live-site pilot feedback revealed expectation capture, layout-risk sensing, and approval-mode gaps that now sit on the critical path for trustworthy agency usage.

slices.title: Project-specific operating profile
slices.goal: Define and implement a lightweight per-project Elementify profile that shapes editing posture, copy density, layout-vs-copy preference, and approval defaults.
acceptance: A typed project profile contract exists for at least editingMode, copyDensity, layoutPriority, changeStyle, and questionPolicy.
Runtime flows can read a project profile and reflect its choices in observable output.
A conservative fallback profile exists when no explicit project profile is present.
The project profile model works for both Free and Advanced without collapsing their surface boundaries.

slices.title: Deterministic layout-risk sensorik
slices.goal: Introduce a deterministic sensing layer that flags likely text-wrapping, density, and spacing risks before or after writes.
acceptance: Current runtime wizard outputs can emit structured layout-risk warnings.
The system can flag at least headline_wrap_risk, card_density_risk, package_card_overflow_risk, and cta_spacing_review.
Risk resolution uses either project-profile defaults or explicit questioning instead of silent truncation/omission.
The first sensorik version is deterministic and covered by tests.

slices.title: Direct, draft, and approval write modes
slices.goal: Add explicit write-mode routing so Elementify can operate in direct-edit, draft-first, or approval-first mode.
acceptance: Runtime flows can declare direct-edit, draft-first, or approval-first behavior.
Draft-first can stage a change without immediate live publication.
Approval-first can create a reviewable change item or bundle with context and risk notes.
Approval behavior can be influenced by project profile and governance settings.
